name: Running - Circular route of the Cueva del Tabaco — Sierra del Mont-roig
name_original: Running - Ruta circular de la Cueva del Tabaco — Sierra del Mont-roig
region_intro: Jogging around Pantà de Camarasa offers diverse terrain influenced by the Central Depression and the Pyrenees. The region features the dramatic Montsec Mountain Range, including Montroig mountain, providing varied landscapes from gentle paths to challenging trails. The Sant Llorenç de Montgai Nature Reserve, with its reservoir and the Segre River, offers scenic waterside running routes. The area's geological formations, such as the Mu Gorge, present unique trail running opportunities. Best jogging routes around Pantà de Camarasa The most popular jogging route is Running - Circular route of the Cueva del Tabaco — Sierra del Mont-roig, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This challenging route explores the Sierra del Mont-roig and passes by the intriguing Tobacco Cave. Another top favourite among local runners is Alòs de Balaguer Spring loop from Presa de Camarasa, a difficult 13.8 miles (22.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local runners also love the Cova del Tabac loop from Presa de Camarasa, a 3.3 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain near the Cova del Tabac, often completed in about 51 minutes. Jogging around Pantà de Camarasa is defined by the Montsec Mountain Range, the Sant Llorenç de Montgai Nature Reserve, and the Pantà de Camarasa reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier paths to more difficult mountain trails. The routes in Pantà de Camarasa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 1200 runners have used komoot to explore Pantà de Camarasa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Pantà de Camarasa?
A: There are over 80 running routes available around Pantà de Camarasa, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Pantà de Camarasa?
A: The terrain around Pantà de Camarasa is incredibly diverse, influenced by both the Central Depression and the Pyrenees. You'll find everything from gentle waterside paths along the Sant Llorenç de Montgai Nature Reserve to challenging trails with significant ascents in the dramatic Montsec Mountain Range. Some routes, like those near the Mu Gorge, even feature unique elements such as wooden bridges and metal walkways attached to cliff faces.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Pantà de Camarasa?
A: Yes, while many routes are challenging, there are at least 2 easy running routes available. These paths offer a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Pantà de Camarasa?
A: The running routes in Pantà de Camarasa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 6 reviews. More than 1200 runners have explored the varied terrain, often praising the scenic views and diverse challenges.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Pantà de Camarasa?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930462460/running-circular-route-of-the-cueva-del-tabaco-sierra-del-mont-roig" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running - Circular route of the Cueva del Tabaco — Sierra del Mont-roig&lt;/a&gt; is a 5.3-mile (8.5 km) trail that explores the Sierra del Mont-roig and passes by the intriguing Tobacco Cave.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the Tobacco Cave?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930462460/running-circular-route-of-the-cueva-del-tabaco-sierra-del-mont-roig" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running - Circular route of the Cueva del Tabaco — Sierra del Mont-roig&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38264829" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova del Tabac loop from Presa de Camarasa&lt;/a&gt; both lead through varied terrain near the Cova del Tabac, a fascinating Bronze Age cavity with an impressive entrance.

Q: Are there challenging trail running options in the Pantà de Camarasa region?
A: Yes, the region is well-suited for challenging trail running. With 65 difficult routes, including those with significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain, experienced runners will find plenty to test their limits. The Montsec Mountain Range and routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14946489" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alòs de Balaguer Spring loop from Presa de Camarasa&lt;/a&gt; offer expansive views and demanding climbs.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Pantà de Camarasa?
A: The area is rich in natural beauty and historical elements. You can encounter the dramatic Montsec Mountain Range, the serene Sant Llorenç de Montgai Nature Reserve, and unique geological formations like the Mu Gorge. Points of interest include the impressive Tobacco Cave, sites with dinosaur traces, the remains of a Roman Bridge, and the Chapel of Sant Jordi perched on a summit.

Q: Are there any routes with significant elevation gain for advanced runners?
A: Definitely. For advanced runners seeking elevation, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31822310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alòs de Balaguer Dam – Views Punta de l'Extrem loop from Presa de Camarasa&lt;/a&gt; offer over 1000 meters of ascent, providing a strenuous workout and rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Can I find running routes that offer great viewpoints?
A: Yes, many trails in the Montsec Mountain Range provide stunning viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31822310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alòs de Balaguer Dam – Views Punta de l'Extrem loop from Presa de Camarasa&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice for expansive vistas, and the Chapel of Sant Jordi also offers a rewarding perspective from its summit.

Q: Are there any longer running routes for endurance training?
A: For endurance training, you can find longer routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31822310" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alòs de Balaguer Dam – Views Punta de l'Extrem loop from Presa de Camarasa&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 31 kilometers (19 miles) and involves significant elevation, making it ideal for longer runs.

Q: What is the best time of year for jogging around Pantà de Camarasa?
A: While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the diverse landscape suggests that spring and autumn would offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer might be warm, making early morning or late afternoon runs preferable, while winter could offer crisp air and unique views, though some higher trails might be affected by weather.
tips: Viewpoint from where you can see beautiful landscapes of La Noguera and Pla de Ponent on the horizon.

At this point we will find the Cova del Tabac, a monumental cave where we will find ancient cave paintings damaged by acts of vandalism.
The cave was formed by karst phenomena, meaning that water infiltrated through the stone over millions of years.
As a curiosity, we know that the name of Cova del Tabac comes from the dust that was extracted from it back in the day to mix it with tobacco.

waypoints.name: View of La Noguera and Pla de Ponent
